proud.en: feeling pleased and satisfied about something you or someone close to you has achieved

ex.en: She was proud of her son's achievement.
ex.ko: 그녀는 아들의 성취를 자랑스러워했다.

ex.en: The old castle stood proud on the hill.
ex.ko: 그 오래된 성은 언덕 위에 당당히 서 있었다.

prove.en: show that something is true by giving facts or evidence

ex.en: Scientists proved the theory through experiments.
ex.ko: 과학자들은 실험을 통해 그 이론을 증명했다.

prove.en: turn out to have a particular quality after a period of time

ex.en: The plan proved to be a huge success.
ex.ko: 그 계획은 엄청난 성공으로 판명되었다.

proverb.en: a short well-known saying that expresses a general truth or piece of advice

ex.en: 'Practice makes perfect' is a common proverb.
ex.ko: '연습이 완벽을 만든다'는 흔한 속담이다.

provided.en: used to say that something will only happen if a particular thing happens or is true

ex.en: You can go out provided you finish your homework.
ex.ko: 숙제를 끝낸다면 나가도 좋다.

psychological.en: relating to the mind or mental processes rather than the body

ex.en: The stress caused several psychological problems.
ex.ko: 그 스트레스는 여러 심리적 문제를 일으켰다.

pub.en: a place, especially in Britain, where people go to drink and meet friends

ex.en: We met at the local pub for a drink.
ex.ko: 우리는 동네 펍에서 만나 한잔했다.

public transport.en: buses, trains, and other vehicles that anyone can use to travel, running on a regular schedule

ex.en: I usually take public transport to work.
ex.ko: 나는 보통 대중교통을 타고 출근한다.

publicly.en: in a way that other people can see or know about

ex.en: He publicly apologized for the mistake.
ex.ko: 그는 공개적으로 그 실수에 대해 사과했다.

publisher.en: a company that produces and sells books, magazines, or newspapers

ex.en: Her novel was accepted by a major publisher.
ex.ko: 그녀의 소설은 대형 출판사에 받아들여졌다.

publisher.en: a person whose job is to publish books, magazines, or newspapers

ex.en: He worked as a publisher for over twenty years.
ex.ko: 그는 20년 넘게 출판인으로 일했다.

pullover.en: a knitted piece of clothing for the upper body that you put on by pulling it over your head

ex.en: He wore a warm pullover in winter.
ex.ko: 그는 겨울에 따뜻한 스웨터를 입었다.

pump.en: a machine that forces liquid or gas to move in a particular direction

ex.en: The water pump broke down last night.
ex.ko: 어젯밤 물 펌프가 고장났다.

pump.en: move liquid or gas somewhere using a pump

ex.en: They pumped water out of the flooded basement.
ex.ko: 그들은 물에 잠긴 지하실에서 물을 퍼냈다.

punish.en: make someone suffer because they have done something wrong

ex.en: The teacher punished the students for cheating.
ex.ko: 선생님은 부정행위를 한 학생들을 처벌했다.

punishment.en: a way of punishing someone for something they have done wrong

ex.en: He received a harsh punishment for the crime.
ex.ko: 그는 그 범죄로 가혹한 처벌을 받았다.

pupil.en: a child or student who is being taught, especially in a school

ex.en: The teacher praised her pupils for their hard work.
ex.ko: 선생님은 제자들의 노력을 칭찬했다.

pupil.en: the small black round opening in the center of the eye that lets in light

ex.en: Her pupils widened in the dark room.
ex.ko: 그녀의 동공은 어두운 방에서 커졌다.

ex.en: They adopted a puppy from the shelter.
ex.ko: 그들은 보호소에서 강아지를 입양했다.

pure.en: not mixed with anything else; clean and unpolluted

ex.en: The mountain air was pure and fresh.
ex.ko: 산속 공기는 순수하고 상쾌했다.

pure.en: complete and total, with no other cause or element involved

ex.en: It was pure luck that we found the keys.
ex.ko: 우리가 열쇠를 찾은 건 순전히 운이었다.

purify.en: remove dirty or harmful substances from something to make it clean

ex.en: This filter purifies the drinking water.
ex.ko: 이 필터는 식수를 정화한다.

purse.en: a small bag used, especially by women, to carry money and personal items

ex.en: She kept her keys in her purse.
ex.ko: 그녀는 열쇠를 핸드백에 넣어 두었다.

puzzle.en: make someone feel confused because they cannot understand something

ex.en: Her strange behavior puzzled everyone.
ex.ko: 그녀의 이상한 행동은 모두를 당혹하게 했다.

puzzle.en: a game or problem that requires careful thought to solve

ex.en: He spent hours solving the jigsaw puzzle.
ex.ko: 그는 직소 퍼즐을 푸느라 몇 시간을 보냈다.

quake.en: a sudden shaking movement of the ground

ex.en: The quake damaged several buildings downtown.
ex.ko: 그 지진은 시내 여러 건물에 피해를 입혔다.

quake.en: shake because you are frightened or nervous

ex.en: He quaked with fear at the sound.
ex.ko: 그는 그 소리에 두려움으로 몸을 떨었다.

qualified.en: having the necessary skills, training, or knowledge to do something

ex.en: She is fully qualified to teach English.
ex.ko: 그녀는 영어를 가르칠 완전한 자격을 갖추고 있다.

qualified.en: limited in some way rather than complete or certain

ex.en: The plan received only qualified support from the board.
ex.ko: 그 계획은 이사회로부터 제한적인 지지만 받았다.

qualify.en: get the right to do something by passing a test or meeting a standard

ex.en: She qualified as a doctor last year.
ex.ko: 그녀는 작년에 의사 자격을 얻었다.

qualify.en: add extra information to a statement to make it less strong or general

ex.en: He qualified his praise by mentioning a few flaws.
ex.ko: 그는 몇 가지 흠을 언급하며 자신의 칭찬에 단서를 달았다.

quantity.en: an amount or number of something that can be measured or counted

ex.en: The factory produces a large quantity of goods each day.
ex.ko: 그 공장은 매일 많은 양의 제품을 생산한다.

question mark.en: the punctuation mark (?) used at the end of a question

ex.en: Don't forget to put a question mark at the end.
ex.ko: 끝에 물음표 넣는 것 잊지 마.

question mark.en: a doubt about whether something is true, certain, or will happen

ex.en: There is still a question mark over his future at the club.
ex.ko: 그 클럽에서 그의 미래에는 여전히 물음표가 남아 있다.

questionnaire.en: a written set of questions used to collect information from people

ex.en: Please fill out this questionnaire about your shopping habits.
ex.ko: 쇼핑 습관에 대한 이 설문지를 작성해 주세요.

queue.en: stand in a line and wait for something

ex.en: We queued for an hour to get tickets.
ex.ko: 우리는 표를 사려고 한 시간 동안 줄을 섰다.

queue.en: a line of people or vehicles waiting for something

ex.en: There was a long queue outside the cinema.
ex.ko: 영화관 밖에 긴 줄이 있었다.

race.en: a competition to see who or what is fastest

ex.en: She won first place in the running race.
ex.ko: 그녀는 달리기 경주에서 1등을 했다.

race.en: take part in a competition to see who is fastest

ex.en: The kids raced each other to the tree.
ex.ko: 아이들은 나무까지 서로 경주했다.

race.en: a group of people who are believed to share the same physical characteristics and ancestry

ex.en: The festival celebrates people of every race and culture.
ex.ko: 그 축제는 모든 인종과 문화의 사람들을 기념한다.

ex.en: The company was accused of racial discrimination.
ex.ko: 그 회사는 인종 차별로 고발되었다.

radiation.en: energy or particles given off by radioactive substances, which can be harmful

ex.en: Workers wore protective suits to avoid radiation.
ex.ko: 작업자들은 방사선을 피하기 위해 보호복을 입었다.

radiation.en: medical treatment for cancer that uses radiation to destroy harmful cells

ex.en: She underwent radiation for six weeks.
ex.ko: 그녀는 6주 동안 방사선 치료를 받았다.

rage.en: a strong feeling of extreme anger that is difficult to control

ex.en: He was shaking with rage after the argument.
ex.ko: 그는 그 말다툼 후 분노로 몸을 떨었다.

rage.en: show extreme anger, or (of a storm etc.) blow with great force

ex.en: He raged at the referee's decision.
ex.ko: 그는 심판의 판정에 격노했다.

rail.en: a bar fixed to a wall or posts that you can hold onto for support

ex.en: Hold onto the rail as you go down the stairs.
ex.ko: 계단을 내려갈 때 난간을 잡으세요.

rail.en: one of the two metal bars that a train runs on

ex.en: The train slowed down as it crossed the rail.
ex.ko: 기차는 선로를 건너면서 속도를 줄였다.

railroad.en: a system of tracks that trains travel on, and the company that runs it (American English)

ex.en: He works for the railroad.
ex.ko: 그는 철도 회사에서 일한다.

railway.en: a system of tracks that trains travel on (British English)

ex.en: They traveled across the country by railway.
ex.ko: 그들은 철도로 전국을 여행했다.

rainfall.en: the amount of rain that falls in a particular place and time

ex.en: This region gets heavy rainfall in summer.
ex.ko: 이 지역은 여름에 강우량이 많다.

rainforest.en: a thick tropical forest with heavy rainfall

ex.en: The Amazon rainforest is home to millions of species.
ex.ko: 아마존 열대우림은 수백만 종의 서식지다.

rank.en: a position in an organization or society that shows how important someone is

ex.en: He rose to the rank of captain.
ex.ko: 그는 대위 계급까지 올랐다.

rank.en: give something or someone a particular position on a scale, or have a position on a scale

ex.en: The team ranks first in the league.
ex.ko: 그 팀은 리그에서 1위를 차지한다.

ex.en: The company has seen rapid growth this year.
ex.ko: 그 회사는 올해 급속한 성장을 이뤘다.

ex.en: Prices rose rapidly last year.
ex.ko: 작년에 물가가 빠르게 올랐다.

ex.en: It's rare to see snow in this city.
ex.ko: 이 도시에서 눈을 보는 것은 드물다.

rare.en: (of meat) cooked for only a short time so it is still red inside

ex.en: He likes his steak rare.
ex.ko: 그는 스테이크를 레어로 익히는 것을 좋아한다.

ex.en: She rarely eats fast food.
ex.ko: 그녀는 좀처럼 패스트푸드를 먹지 않는다.

raspberry.en: a small soft red fruit that grows on a bush

ex.en: She added raspberries to her yogurt.
ex.ko: 그녀는 요거트에 라즈베리를 넣었다.

ration.en: a fixed amount of food or goods that each person is allowed, especially when supplies are limited

ex.en: During the war, each family received a weekly ration of sugar.
ex.ko: 전쟁 중에 각 가정은 매주 설탕 배급을 받았다.

ration.en: limit the amount of something each person can have, especially when it is scarce

ex.en: The government rationed fuel during the crisis.
ex.ko: 정부는 위기 동안 연료를 배급제로 제한했다.

rational.en: based on clear thought and reason rather than emotion

ex.en: We need to make a rational decision about this.
ex.ko: 우리는 이 문제에 대해 합리적인 결정을 내려야 한다.

razor.en: a tool with a sharp blade used for shaving hair from the skin

ex.en: He shaved with a new razor this morning.
ex.ko: 그는 오늘 아침 새 면도기로 면도했다.

react.en: behave or respond in a particular way because of something that happens

ex.en: How did she react to the news?
ex.ko: 그녀는 그 소식에 어떻게 반응했나요?

react.en: undergo a chemical change when combined with another substance

ex.en: Sodium reacts violently with water.
ex.ko: 나트륨은 물과 격렬하게 반응한다.

realistic.en: seeing or accepting things as they really are, not as you wish they were

ex.en: We need to be realistic about our chances of winning.
ex.ko: 우리는 이길 가능성에 대해 현실적이어야 한다.

reality.en: the way things actually are, as opposed to how you might imagine or wish them to be

ex.en: His dream finally became a reality.
ex.ko: 그의 꿈은 마침내 현실이 되었다.

rearrange.en: change the order or position of things

ex.en: She rearranged the furniture in her room.
ex.ko: 그녀는 방 안의 가구를 재배열했다.

reasonable.en: fair and sensible, based on good judgment

ex.en: That seems like a reasonable explanation.
ex.ko: 그것은 타당한 설명처럼 보인다.

reasonable.en: (of a price) not too expensive; fair

ex.en: The hotel offers rooms at a reasonable price.
ex.ko: 그 호텔은 합리적인 가격에 객실을 제공한다.

rebuild.en: build something again after it has been damaged or destroyed

ex.en: The town was rebuilt after the earthquake.
ex.ko: 그 마을은 지진 이후 재건되었다.

recall.en: an order to return a faulty product to the maker for repair or replacement

ex.en: The carmaker announced a recall of thousands of vehicles.
ex.ko: 그 자동차 회사는 수천 대의 차량에 대한 회수를 발표했다.

recall.en: bring information back into your mind

ex.en: She couldn't recall where she had left her keys.
ex.ko: 그녀는 열쇠를 어디에 뒀는지 기억해내지 못했다.

reception.en: a formal party held to welcome someone or celebrate an event

ex.en: They held a wedding reception at the hotel.
ex.ko: 그들은 호텔에서 결혼 피로연을 열었다.

reception.en: the area in a hotel or office where visitors are greeted and helped

ex.en: Please leave your key at reception.
ex.ko: 열쇠는 접수처에 맡겨 주세요.

recite.en: say a poem or piece of writing aloud from memory

ex.en: The student recited a poem in front of the class.
ex.ko: 그 학생은 반 친구들 앞에서 시를 암송했다.

reclaim.en: get something back that was lost, taken away, or given up

ex.en: He tried to reclaim his lost luggage at the airport.
ex.ko: 그는 공항에서 잃어버린 짐을 되찾으려 했다.

recognize.en: know someone or something because you have seen or experienced them before

ex.en: I almost didn't recognize her with short hair.
ex.ko: 나는 그녀가 머리를 짧게 잘라서 거의 못 알아볼 뻔했다.

recognize.en: formally accept that something is true, official, or deserves respect

ex.en: The government finally recognized the union.
ex.ko: 정부는 마침내 그 노조를 인정했다.

recommend.en: suggest that someone or something is good or suitable for a particular purpose

ex.en: Can you recommend a good restaurant nearby?
ex.ko: 근처에 좋은 식당 좀 추천해 줄래요?

recording.en: a piece of music, sound, or video that has been recorded

ex.en: They listened to a recording of the concert.
ex.ko: 그들은 그 콘서트의 녹음을 들었다.

recover.en: become well again after being ill, injured, or upset

ex.en: It took months for her to recover from the surgery.
ex.ko: 그녀가 수술에서 회복하는 데 몇 달이 걸렸다.

recover.en: get something back that was lost or taken away

ex.en: Police recovered the stolen jewelry.
ex.ko: 경찰은 도난당한 보석을 되찾았다.

recovery.en: the process of becoming well or normal again after illness, damage, or difficulty

ex.en: The economy is showing signs of recovery.
ex.ko: 경제가 회복의 조짐을 보이고 있다.

recycled.en: made from materials that have been used before and processed again

ex.en: This notebook is made of recycled paper.
ex.ko: 이 공책은 재활용 종이로 만들어졌다.

recycling.cue: 쓰레기를 다시 쓸 수 있게 처리
recycling.en: the process of treating used materials so they can be used again

ex.en: Our city has a strong recycling program.
ex.ko: 우리 도시는 재활용 프로그램이 잘 되어 있다.

reduce.en: make something smaller in size, amount, or degree

ex.en: We need to reduce our spending this month.
ex.ko: 우리는 이번 달 지출을 줄여야 한다.

reduction.en: the act of making something smaller in size, amount, or degree

ex.en: The company announced a reduction in prices.
ex.ko: 그 회사는 가격 인하를 발표했다.

reef.cue: 바다 표면 가까이 있는 바위·산호 줄기
reef.en: a long line of rock or coral near the surface of the sea

ex.en: The boat almost hit the reef.
ex.ko: 그 배는 암초에 부딪힐 뻔했다.

referee.en: an official who makes sure players follow the rules in a sports game

ex.en: The referee blew the whistle for a foul.
ex.ko: 심판은 반칙에 대해 호루라기를 불었다.

referee.cue: 취업 지원 시 자신을 보증해줄 사람
referee.en: a person who provides a statement about your character or qualifications, especially for a job application

ex.en: Please provide the names of two referees on your application.
ex.ko: 지원서에 신원보증인 두 명의 이름을 적어 주세요.

reflection.en: an image of something seen in a mirror or shiny surface, or light bouncing back from a surface

ex.en: She looked at her reflection in the mirror.
ex.ko: 그녀는 거울에 비친 자기 모습을 보았다.

reflection.en: careful thought about something, especially in the past

ex.en: After some reflection, she decided to change her career.
ex.ko: 얼마간의 성찰 끝에 그녀는 진로를 바꾸기로 결심했다.

refreshments.en: light food and drinks provided at an event or meeting

ex.en: Refreshments will be served after the ceremony.
ex.ko: 행사 후에 다과가 제공될 예정이다.

refund.en: an amount of money that is given back to you, especially because you returned goods

ex.en: I asked for a refund on the broken item.
ex.ko: 나는 그 고장난 물건에 대해 환불을 요청했다.

ex.en: The store refunded my money without any questions.
ex.ko: 그 가게는 아무 질문 없이 내 돈을 환불해 주었다.

refusal.en: the act of saying no to something that is offered or asked

ex.en: Her refusal to apologize surprised everyone.
ex.ko: 그녀가 사과를 거절한 것은 모두를 놀라게 했다.

refuse.en: say no to something that is offered, requested, or suggested

ex.en: He refused to answer the question.
ex.ko: 그는 그 질문에 답하기를 거절했다.

refuse.en: waste material that is thrown away (a formal word, pronounced differently from the verb)

ex.en: The refuse was collected early in the morning.
ex.ko: 쓰레기는 아침 일찍 수거되었다.

regain.en: get something back after losing it

ex.en: She regained consciousness after a few minutes.
ex.ko: 그녀는 몇 분 후 의식을 되찾았다.

regard.en: a particular aspect or detail of something being discussed

ex.en: In this regard, the new policy is a big improvement.
ex.ko: 이 점에서 새 정책은 큰 개선이다.

regard.en: think of someone or something in a particular way

ex.en: Many people regard her as the best singer of her generation.
ex.ko: 많은 사람들이 그녀를 그 세대 최고의 가수로 여긴다.

regard.en: a friendly greeting sent to someone, especially at the end of a letter or message

ex.en: Please give my regards to your family.
ex.ko: 가족분들께 안부 전해 주세요.

regarding.en: used to say what subject you are talking or writing about

ex.en: I have a question regarding the schedule.
ex.ko: 일정에 관해 질문이 있어요.

region.en: a particular area of a country or the world

ex.en: This region is famous for its wine.
ex.ko: 이 지역은 와인으로 유명하다.

ex.en: The festival features regional food and music.
ex.ko: 그 축제는 지역 음식과 음악을 특징으로 한다.

register.en: put your name on an official list for something

ex.en: You need to register for the course online.
ex.ko: 그 강좌에 등록하려면 온라인으로 해야 한다.

register.en: an official written list of names, events, or information

ex.en: The teacher checked the attendance register.
ex.ko: 선생님은 출석부를 확인했다.

registration.en: the process of officially recording your name on a list

ex.en: Registration for the marathon closes next week.
ex.ko: 마라톤 등록은 다음 주에 마감된다.

regulation.en: an official rule that controls how something is done

ex.en: The factory must follow strict safety regulations.
ex.ko: 그 공장은 엄격한 안전 규정을 따라야 한다.

reject.en: refuse to accept, agree to, or consider something

ex.en: The committee rejected his proposal.
ex.ko: 위원회는 그의 제안을 거부했다.

relate.en: show or make a connection between two or more things

ex.en: It's hard to relate these two events.
ex.ko: 이 두 사건을 연관짓기는 어렵다.

relate.en: understand and feel sympathy for someone's feelings or experiences

ex.en: I really relate to what she said about stress.
ex.ko: 나는 그녀가 스트레스에 대해 한 말에 정말 공감한다.

relation.en: the way in which two or more things or people are connected

ex.en: There is a clear relation between diet and health.
ex.ko: 식단과 건강 사이에는 분명한 관계가 있다.

relationship.en: the way in which two or more people or things are connected, or a close emotional connection between people

ex.en: They have a close relationship with their neighbors.
ex.ko: 그들은 이웃과 가까운 관계를 맺고 있다.

ex.en: She visited her relatives during the holidays.
ex.ko: 그녀는 명절 동안 친척들을 방문했다.

relative.en: considered in comparison with something else

ex.en: The relative cost of living here is quite high.
ex.ko: 이곳의 상대적인 생활비는 꽤 높다.

relatively.en: in comparison with other things or with what you might expect

ex.en: The exam was relatively easy this year.
ex.ko: 올해 시험은 비교적 쉬웠다.

relaxing.en: helping you feel calm and free from stress

ex.en: We spent a relaxing weekend at the beach.
ex.ko: 우리는 해변에서 편안한 주말을 보냈다.

reliable.en: able to be trusted to do what you expect or need

ex.en: He is a reliable friend who always keeps his word.
ex.ko: 그는 항상 약속을 지키는 믿을 수 있는 친구다.

religion.en: a belief in a god or gods, and the practices connected with this belief

ex.en: People of different religions live together peacefully in this city.
ex.ko: 이 도시에서는 다른 종교를 가진 사람들이 평화롭게 함께 산다.

religious.en: relating to religion, or having strong religious beliefs

ex.en: She comes from a very religious family.
ex.ko: 그녀는 매우 종교적인 가정 출신이다.

rely.en: depend on someone or something to do or provide what is needed

ex.en: She relies on public transport to get to work.
ex.ko: 그녀는 출근하는 데 대중교통에 의존한다.

remainder.en: the part of something that is left after the rest has been used, removed, or dealt with

ex.en: He spent the remainder of his life in the countryside.
ex.ko: 그는 남은 생을 시골에서 보냈다.

remark.en: say something briefly, especially to express an opinion

ex.en: She remarked that the food was excellent.
ex.ko: 그녀는 음식이 훌륭하다고 말했다.

remark.en: something you say that expresses an opinion or reaction

ex.en: His remark about her work upset her.
ex.ko: 그녀의 작업에 대한 그의 발언은 그녀를 화나게 했다.

remarkable.en: unusual or impressive enough to deserve attention

ex.en: She made a remarkable recovery after the accident.
ex.ko: 그녀는 사고 후 놀라운 회복을 보였다.

remedy.en: a medicine or treatment that cures an illness or relieves pain

ex.en: Honey and lemon is a popular home remedy for colds.
ex.ko: 꿀과 레몬은 감기에 인기 있는 민간요법이다.

remedy.en: a way of solving a problem or improving a bad situation

ex.en: The government proposed a remedy for the housing crisis.
ex.ko: 정부는 주택 위기에 대한 해결책을 제안했다.

remembrance.en: the ability or act of remembering past events

ex.en: She smiled in remembrance of their trip together.
ex.ko: 그녀는 함께한 여행을 기억하며 미소지었다.

remembrance.en: a ceremony or act that honors someone who has died

ex.en: They held a service in remembrance of the victims.
ex.ko: 그들은 희생자들을 추모하는 예배를 열었다.

remote control.en: a device used to operate a machine such as a TV from a distance

ex.en: Where did I put the remote control?
ex.ko: 리모컨을 어디에 뒀지?

remove.en: take something away or off from a place

ex.en: Please remove your shoes before entering.
ex.ko: 들어오기 전에 신발을 벗어 주세요.

renew.en: make something valid again or start it again

ex.en: I need to renew my passport this year.
ex.ko: 나는 올해 여권을 갱신해야 한다.

repay.en: pay back money that you owe, or do something in return for a favor

ex.en: It will take years to repay the loan.
ex.ko: 그 대출을 갚는 데 몇 년이 걸릴 것이다.

ex.en: He repeatedly asked her to marry him.
ex.ko: 그는 그녀에게 반복해서 청혼했다.

representative.en: a person chosen to speak or act on behalf of a group

ex.en: A union representative met with the managers.
ex.ko: 노조 대표가 관리자들과 만났다.

representative.en: typical of a particular group or class of things

ex.en: This painting is representative of his early work.
ex.ko: 이 그림은 그의 초기 작품을 대표적으로 보여준다.

reproduce.en: produce offspring or new individuals of the same kind

ex.en: Rabbits reproduce very quickly.
ex.ko: 토끼는 매우 빨리 번식한다.

reproduce.en: make an exact copy of something

ex.en: The museum reproduced the painting for the exhibition.
ex.ko: 박물관은 전시를 위해 그 그림을 복제했다.

republic.en: a country governed by elected representatives rather than a king or queen

ex.en: France became a republic after the revolution.
ex.ko: 프랑스는 혁명 후 공화국이 되었다.

reputation.cue: 사람들이 갖고 있는 어떤 대상에 대한 평가
reputation.en: the general opinion that people have about someone or something

ex.en: The restaurant has a good reputation for quality food.
ex.ko: 그 식당은 좋은 음식으로 평판이 좋다.

require.en: need something or make something necessary

ex.en: This job requires excellent communication skills.
ex.ko: 이 일은 뛰어난 의사소통 능력을 필요로 한다.

requirement.en: something that is needed or must be done

ex.en: A college degree is a requirement for this position.
ex.ko: 이 직책에는 대학 학위가 필요조건이다.

rescue.en: save someone from danger or a difficult situation

ex.en: Firefighters rescued the family from the burning house.
ex.ko: 소방관들은 불타는 집에서 그 가족을 구조했다.

rescue.en: the act of saving someone from danger

ex.en: The rescue took several hours.
ex.ko: 그 구조는 몇 시간이 걸렸다.

researcher.en: a person who studies a subject in detail to discover new information

ex.en: Researchers are studying the effects of climate change.
ex.ko: 연구원들은 기후 변화의 영향을 연구하고 있다.

resemble.en: look like or be similar to someone or something

ex.en: She resembles her mother a lot.
ex.ko: 그녀는 어머니를 많이 닮았다.

reservation.en: an arrangement to have a seat, table, or room kept for you

ex.en: I made a reservation for two at seven o'clock.
ex.ko: 나는 7시에 두 명 예약을 했다.

reservation.en: a feeling of doubt that stops you from fully agreeing with or trusting something

ex.en: I still have some reservations about the plan.
ex.ko: 나는 여전히 그 계획에 대해 약간의 의구심이 있다.

reserve.en: arrange in advance to have something such as a table or seat

ex.en: We reserved a table at the restaurant.
ex.ko: 우리는 그 식당에 테이블을 예약했다.

reserve.en: keep something for future use instead of using it now

ex.en: They reserved some food for the next day.
ex.ko: 그들은 다음 날을 위해 음식을 좀 남겨두었다.

reserve.en: an amount of something kept for future use

ex.en: The country has large oil reserves.
ex.ko: 그 나라는 많은 석유 비축량을 가지고 있다.

resident.en: a person who lives in a particular place

ex.en: Local residents complained about the noise.
ex.ko: 지역 주민들은 소음에 대해 항의했다.

resident.en: living or staying in a particular place, especially on a permanent or regular basis

ex.en: The hotel has a resident chef who prepares all the meals.
ex.ko: 그 호텔에는 모든 식사를 준비하는 상주 셰프가 있다.

residential.en: relating to areas where people live, as opposed to work or shops

ex.en: They live in a quiet residential neighborhood.
ex.ko: 그들은 조용한 주거 지역에 산다.

resist.en: refuse to accept something or fight against it

ex.en: The villagers resisted the new construction plan.
ex.ko: 마을 주민들은 새 건설 계획에 저항했다.

resist.en: stop yourself from doing or having something you want

ex.en: I couldn't resist eating the last piece of cake.
ex.ko: 나는 마지막 케이크 조각을 먹고 싶은 걸 참을 수 없었다.

resolve.en: find a solution to a problem or disagreement

ex.en: They resolved the dispute peacefully.
ex.ko: 그들은 그 분쟁을 평화롭게 해결했다.

ex.en: She faced the challenge with great resolve.
ex.ko: 그녀는 대단한 결의로 그 도전에 맞섰다.

resort.cue: 다른 방법이 없어 어쩔 수 없이 어떤 수단에
resort.en: turn to and make use of something, especially as a last option

ex.en: The company had to resort to layoffs to save money.
ex.ko: 그 회사는 돈을 아끼기 위해 어쩔 수 없이 정리해고에 의지해야 했다.

resort.en: a place where people go on vacation to relax

ex.en: They spent a week at a beach resort.
ex.ko: 그들은 해변 리조트에서 일주일을 보냈다.

resource.cue: 필요할 때 쓸 수 있는 것
resource.en: something valuable that can be used to help you achieve something, such as money, materials, or people

ex.en: Water is a precious natural resource.
ex.ko: 물은 귀중한 천연자원이다.

respect.en: a feeling of admiration for someone because of their qualities or achievements

ex.en: Students should treat their teachers with respect.
ex.ko: 학생들은 선생님을 존중하는 태도로 대해야 한다.

respect.en: admire someone because of their qualities or achievements, and treat them well

ex.en: I really respect her for her honesty.
ex.ko: 나는 그녀의 정직함을 정말 존경한다.

respectable.cue: 사회적으로 인정받을 만큼 훌륭하고 예의 바른
respectable.en: considered to be honest, decent, and socially acceptable

ex.en: He comes from a respectable family.
ex.ko: 그는 훌륭한 가문 출신이다.

respond.en: give an answer or reaction to something

ex.en: She responded quickly to my email.
ex.ko: 그녀는 내 이메일에 빠르게 응답했다.

responsibility.en: a duty to deal with or take care of something, so that it is your fault if something goes wrong

ex.en: Taking care of the dog is your responsibility now.
ex.ko: 이제 그 개를 돌보는 것은 네 책임이다.

responsible.en: having a duty to deal with or take care of something

ex.en: She is responsible for the whole project.
ex.ko: 그녀는 그 프로젝트 전체를 책임지고 있다.

responsible.en: being the cause of something, especially something bad

ex.en: The storm was responsible for the power outage.
ex.ko: 그 폭풍이 정전의 원인이었다.

rest.en: a period of relaxing or sleeping after activity

ex.en: You need some rest after that long trip.
ex.ko: 그 긴 여행 후에는 좀 쉬어야 해.

rest.en: stop doing something in order to relax or sleep

ex.en: Let's rest here for a few minutes.
ex.ko: 여기서 잠깐 쉬자.

rest.en: the part of something that is left after the rest has been dealt with

ex.en: I'll read the rest of the book tonight.
ex.ko: 나머지 책은 오늘 밤에 읽을 거야.

restore.en: return something, such as an old building or artwork, to its original condition

ex.en: They restored the old castle to its former glory.
ex.ko: 그들은 그 오래된 성을 예전 모습으로 복원했다.

restore.en: bring back a situation, feeling, or condition that existed before

ex.en: The new policy restored public confidence in the government.
ex.ko: 그 새 정책은 정부에 대한 국민의 신뢰를 회복시켰다.

restrict.en: keep something within a limited amount, range, or extent

ex.en: The doctor restricted his diet because of his blood pressure.
ex.ko: 의사는 그의 혈압 때문에 식단을 제한했다.

retail.en: the selling of goods directly to customers, usually in shops, as opposed to in bulk

ex.en: The company makes most of its profit from retail, not wholesale.
ex.ko: 그 회사는 도매가 아니라 소매에서 대부분의 이익을 낸다.

retail.en: sell goods directly to consumers, often at a stated price

ex.en: The jacket retails for about eighty dollars.
ex.ko: 그 재킷은 소매가로 약 80달러에 팔린다.

retain.en: continue to have or keep something

ex.en: She managed to retain her title as champion.
ex.ko: 그녀는 챔피언 타이틀을 지켜냈다.

ex.en: It's hard to retain so many new words at once.
ex.ko: 한꺼번에 그렇게 많은 새 단어를 기억하기는 어렵다.

retell.en: tell a story again, often in a different way

ex.en: The movie retells the story of Cinderella in a modern setting.
ex.ko: 그 영화는 신데렐라 이야기를 현대적 배경으로 다시 들려준다.

retrospect.cue: 지난 일을 지금 돌이켜 볼 때
retrospect.en: a look back at or review of past events, often used in the phrase 'in retrospect'

ex.en: In retrospect, quitting that job was the right decision.
ex.ko: 돌이켜보면, 그 일을 그만둔 것은 옳은 결정이었다.

reunification.en: the process of bringing separated parts of a country or group back together as one

ex.en: Many still hope for the reunification of the two Koreas.
ex.ko: 많은 사람들이 여전히 남북한의 재통일을 바란다.

reunify.en: bring separated parts of a country or group back together as one

ex.en: The two nations worked for decades to reunify.
ex.ko: 그 두 나라는 재통일을 위해 수십 년간 노력했다.

revise.en: change something, especially a piece of writing or a plan, in order to improve or correct it

ex.en: She revised her essay before submitting it.
ex.ko: 그녀는 에세이를 제출하기 전에 수정했다.

revise.en: study material you have already learned again, especially to prepare for an exam (British English)

ex.en: I need to revise for my chemistry exam this weekend.
ex.ko: 이번 주말에 화학 시험 공부(복습)를 해야 해.

revision.en: a change made to something, especially a text or plan, to improve or correct it

ex.en: The final revision of the report was submitted on Friday.
ex.ko: 보고서의 최종 수정본이 금요일에 제출되었다.

revision.cue: 시험 준비로 배운 내용을 다시 봄 (영국식)
revision.en: the process of studying something again to prepare for an exam (British English)

ex.en: She spent the weekend doing revision for her exams.
ex.ko: 그녀는 주말 내내 시험 복습을 하며 보냈다.

reward.en: give something to someone in return for good behavior, hard work, or achievement

ex.en: The company rewarded her hard work with a promotion.
ex.ko: 그 회사는 그녀의 노고를 승진으로 보상했다.

reward.en: something given in return for good behavior, hard work, or achievement

ex.en: Getting the job was the reward for months of preparation.
ex.ko: 그 일자리를 얻은 것은 몇 달간의 준비에 대한 보상이었다.

rewrite.en: write something again in a different or better way

ex.en: He rewrote the introduction to make it clearer.
ex.ko: 그는 서론을 더 명확하게 만들려고 다시 썼다.

rid.cue: 원치 않는 것을 없애다 (주로 get rid of)
rid.en: free someone or something of an unwanted thing, especially in the phrase 'get rid of'

ex.en: We finally got rid of the old furniture.
ex.ko: 우리는 마침내 낡은 가구를 없앴다.

riddle.en: a puzzling question or statement that has a clever or funny answer

ex.en: She asked me a riddle about a clock.
ex.ko: 그녀는 나에게 시계에 관한 수수께끼를 냈다.

riddle.en: something that is very difficult to understand or explain

ex.en: The disappearance remains a riddle to this day.
ex.ko: 그 실종 사건은 오늘날까지도 수수께끼로 남아 있다.

ridiculous.en: deserving to be laughed at because it is very silly or unreasonable

ex.en: It's ridiculous to expect me to finish this in one day.
ex.ko: 이걸 하루 만에 끝내라는 건 터무니없다.

ripe.en: (of fruit or crops) fully grown and ready to eat

ex.en: These bananas are perfectly ripe.
ex.ko: 이 바나나들은 딱 알맞게 익었다.

ripe.en: at the right stage or time for something to happen

ex.en: The moment seemed ripe for a change in strategy.
ex.ko: 전략을 바꾸기에 딱 알맞은 시점인 것 같았다.

ex.en: Smoke rose from the chimney.
ex.ko: 굴뚝에서 연기가 피어올랐다.

rise.en: increase in amount, number, or value

ex.en: House prices have risen sharply this year.
ex.ko: 올해 집값이 급격히 올랐다.

rise.en: get up from a sitting, kneeling, or lying position

ex.en: He rose from his chair to greet the guests.
ex.ko: 그는 손님들을 맞이하러 의자에서 일어났다.

rise.en: an increase in amount, number, or level

ex.en: There was a sudden rise in temperature.
ex.ko: 기온이 갑자기 상승했다.

risk.en: do something even though it might result in something bad or dangerous

ex.en: She risked her career by speaking out against the policy.
ex.ko: 그녀는 그 정책에 반대 목소리를 내면서 자신의 경력을 위험에 빠뜨렸다.

risk.en: the possibility that something bad or dangerous might happen

ex.en: Smoking increases the risk of heart disease.
ex.ko: 흡연은 심장병 위험을 높인다.

roadside.en: the strip of land along the edge of a road

ex.en: They stopped to rest at the roadside.
ex.ko: 그들은 길가에 멈춰 쉬었다.

robot.en: a machine that can perform tasks automatically, often controlled by a computer

ex.en: The factory uses robots to assemble cars.
ex.ko: 그 공장은 자동차 조립에 로봇을 사용한다.

rolling.en: moving by turning over and over, like a wheel or a ball

ex.en: A rolling stone gathers no moss.
ex.ko: 구르는 돌에는 이끼가 끼지 않는다.

rolling.en: (of land) rising and falling gently in a series of smooth slopes

ex.en: The rolling hills stretched for miles.
ex.ko: 완만하게 물결치는 언덕이 몇 마일이나 이어졌다.

rotten.en: decayed and no longer fit to eat or use

ex.en: The apples at the bottom of the bag were rotten.
ex.ko: 가방 맨 아래에 있던 사과들은 썩어 있었다.

ex.en: I've had a rotten day at work.
ex.ko: 오늘 회사에서 끔찍한 하루를 보냈다.

rough.en: having an uneven or bumpy surface; not smooth

ex.en: The rough path made walking difficult.
ex.ko: 그 거친 길은 걷기 힘들게 만들었다.

rough.en: not gentle or careful in behavior; harsh

ex.en: He can be a bit rough with his younger brother.
ex.ko: 그는 남동생에게 좀 거칠게 대할 때가 있다.

ex.en: Can you give me a rough estimate of the cost?
ex.ko: 비용에 대해 대략적인 견적을 줄 수 있어?

rough.en: difficult or unpleasant to deal with

ex.en: She's been through a rough time lately.
ex.ko: 그녀는 요즘 힘든 시기를 겪고 있다.

routine.en: the usual series of things you do at a particular time

ex.en: Exercising every morning is part of his daily routine.
ex.ko: 매일 아침 운동하는 것은 그의 일상의 일부다.

routine.en: done regularly as a normal part of a process; not unusual or special

ex.en: It was just a routine check-up at the doctor's.
ex.ko: 그건 그냥 병원에서의 정기 검진일 뿐이었다.

rubber.en: a strong, elastic material used to make tires, shoes, and other products

ex.en: The soles of these shoes are made of rubber.
ex.ko: 이 신발의 밑창은 고무로 만들어졌다.

rubber.en: a small piece of rubber used to remove pencil marks (British English)

ex.en: Can I borrow your rubber to fix this mistake?
ex.ko: 이 실수 고치게 지우개 좀 빌려줄래?

rubbish.en: waste material that is thrown away (British English)

ex.en: Please put the rubbish in the bin.
ex.ko: 쓰레기는 쓰레기통에 넣어 주세요.

rubbish.en: nonsense; something that is not true or sensible

ex.en: Don't talk rubbish — of course I remember your birthday.
ex.ko: 허튼소리 하지 마, 당연히 네 생일 기억하지.

rudely.en: in a way that is not polite

ex.en: He rudely interrupted her while she was speaking.
ex.ko: 그는 그녀가 말하는 도중에 무례하게 끼어들었다.

runaway.en: a person, especially a young one, who has left home or an institution without permission

ex.en: The shelter helps runaways find a safe place to stay.
ex.ko: 그 보호소는 가출 청소년들이 안전한 곳을 찾도록 돕는다.

runaway.en: increasing or moving quickly in a way that cannot be controlled

ex.en: The government struggled to deal with runaway inflation.
ex.ko: 정부는 걷잡을 수 없는 인플레이션에 대처하느라 애를 먹었다.
